title: Spiced Roasted Red Pepper Couscous
description: An earthy and gooey Irish-inspired dish featuring roasted red peppers with a touch of spice, perfect for festive meals and enjoyed as a side or main course.

ingredients.name: 1 large red bell pepper, stemmed and seeded
4 dried red chillies (or to taste)
ingredients.notes: Soaked in hot water for 30 mins

instructions: Preheat your oven to 400°F (205°C). Rinse the couscous under cold water.
Prepare the red bell pepper by roasting it until tender, roughly 10-15 minutes on a baking sheet. Allow to cool before chopping into chunks.
In a frying pan, sauté the soaked and chopped dried chillies with olive oil for about 2 minutes or until fragrant.
Melt the unsalted butter in another pan over low heat and add the roasted red bell peppers, lemon zest, and cooled couscous. Stir well to combine all ingredients.
Pour the combined mixture into an oven-safe dish. Top with the sautéed chillies and pour hot water over the couscous to cover (about 1/2 cup per cup of couscous).
Bake for about 20-25 minutes or until the top layer is golden brown.
Serve warm, garnished with extra lemon zest if desired.
